rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAKISHA WILSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Allowance Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/21. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The 29th accounting for the period 7/1/2019 to 6/30/2021 has not been filed. The Court failed to set a status date in the order settling the 28th accounting, but it is the trustee's responsibility, in any event, to make sure to file its accountings timely. Once the accounting petition is filed, this petition will be continued to the same date as that hearing. [LRSF 14.56 B.] Since the accounting period will end 6/30/21, if Counsel wishes to request fees through the accounting period (or even later, to the date of filing the accounting petition), either an amendment to this petition or a fully amended fee petition could be filed. Notice is required for any amending pleading. [CRC 7.53(a).] Attorney costs show $5.00 E-filing costs twice on 11/1/19 for the "status report" filed on 10/2/19. It is unclear why the usual $5.00 E-filing cost is sometimes higher: see $10 payment for the Notice of Hearing filed on 10/09/19; and $20 payment for two Notices of Hearing filed on 2/13/20 (there was none filed on 2/11/20). There is no statutory need to give notice of a "reset" (continued) hearing. [Prob. C. Sec. 1205.] Timesheets have not yet been fully reviewed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TOK CHA VAN SANT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/8/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The petition caption does not include the request for additional time to administer the estate. [CRC 7.102.] Schedule A (Receipts) does not show the source of the receipts and Schedule B (Disbursements) does not identify all of the payees. [Probate Code Section 1062(a).] Supporting schedules should have four columns to show the date, source or payee, description, and amount.) The petition does not address the characterization of the property (not just "personal" property, but whether it is separate property). The prayer does not request a specific date, so "through 2021" presumably means through December 2021. (Paragraph 8 should read: "because neither... has any knowledge of an heir or beneficiary being held or having been held." This is not an "annual" account.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/29/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD CHESTER PRATT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator requested 120 days of additional time to administer the estate on 3/22/21. 2) Notice was not mailed to the decedent's surviving spouse, Charlene Pitchford, only to her attorney. Notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] This was posted in the tentative rulings but still not addressed. 3) An unfiled copy of the administrator's attorney's declaration was mailed to this Examiner on 9/8/21. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Notwithstanding the copy not being a proper file stamped courtesy copy, assuming it is the same as the declaration filed on 9/8/21, the Court will have to determine if it should be considered, as allegations were made by the attorney, not the fiduciary. [CRC 7.103(c).] Furthermore, no waiver and acknowledgment signed by Charlene Pitchford has been filed yet. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHIKO KAWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The two children of the decedent's predeceased spouse entitled to distribution signed assignments of 1/3 of their respective shares of the estate to Hoerner Bank in Germany. W.C. Cox & Co. appeared through its counsel in this proceeding as the intended assignee by filing a notice of partial assignments. The assignments signed by the two children do not identify W.C. Cox. No limited power of attorney signed by the two children to authorize W.C. Cox & Co. or its counsel to receive distribution is on file. Although Hoerner Bank's waiver of notice of this hearing is on file, it is unclear whether the bank consented to direct distribution of the assigned shares to W.C. Cox & Co. or its counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AURA P. HUCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar for failure to respond to issues posted in the tentative rulings (no courtesy copy, no proposed order) and a new status hearing be set for closing the estate. Issues: 1) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] 2) Paragraph 4 is unclear as to whether or not the creditor's claim was actually allowed and paid. No Allowance or Rejetion of that claim was filed, as required by Probate Code Section 9250(b). 3) The petition also does not address whether or not the Probate Referee's commission (or any other expense of the estate) was paid. 4) If the creditor and the Probate Referee were not paid, they must receive notice. 5) The proof of service of notice shows that notice was mailed on 6/14/21. Although the petition is file stamped 6/14/21, due to this Court's shortage of clerks, the petition was not actually e-filed (available for viewing) until 6/16/21. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 6) Curiously, neither the initial Petition for Probate filed on 9/20/19 nor the supplement thereto filed on 10/15/19 addressed why the executor's brother, Axel Joachim Huck, was not listed. He is named as one of the contingent residual beneficiaries in the devisee trust, along with Decedent's five siblings. 7) A Declaration of Real Property was filed at the outset of these proceedings (on 10/15/19), indicating that Decedent's 50% interests in real property would pass to the surviving spouse, but no real property was inventoried. Despite the reference to Probate Code Sections 17200.1 and 850 in the petition caption, this actually appears to be a petition under Probate Code Section 12251. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OMAR ABDULLAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/08/2021. Still no filed response after multiple continuances. 1) Please explain why the attorney requests reimbursement of a $540.00 filing fee for General Conservatorship Petition on page 6 line 1 of the petition when the filing fee paid was $450.00. 2) Please file conservator timesheets that conform to LRSF 14.53. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. 3) Please explain and provide legal basis for why the conservator is requesting fees for tolls and mileage when LRSF 14.58 says tolls and mileage are absobed as part of fees. It appears this needs to be amended to remove the request for tolls and mileage. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/2021. 1) The caption is for a Petition for Amened Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. Petitioner selected on page 1 #2 b. (3) administrator. Please explain. 2) Petitioner did not select a required box on page 3 # 5.a. (3) or (4). 3) If a citizen of a foreign country dies without leaving a will or leaves a will without naming an executor, or if it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. (Probate Code 8113). 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/29/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF XIU CHAN MEI RUAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Except as otherwise permitted in the Probate Code, a notice sent by mail under Probate Code section 1220 must be mailed individually and directly to the person entitled to notice. [CRC 7.51(a)(1).] If a party is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney, unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to sent the notice only in care of the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1214 and CRC 7.51(a)(2).] A Request for Special Notice is not written direction that notice be mailed only to the attorney representing heirs. The mothers of the minor heirs waived bond. They were not appointed as guardians ad litem and nothing has been filed to confirm that they are Court-appointed guardians of the estate of their minor child/children. The Court to determine if a guardian ad litem should be appointed to represent the minors' interests at this stage in the estate proceeding. (In any event, a guardian of the estate will likely need to be appointed before any distribution is made to the minors. [LRSF 14.36 E.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KALVIN M. MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/8/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., B., and C., and 14.60 E.] Notwithstanding the local rules, the petition was cursorily reviewed online as a ONE-TIME COURTESY and the following issues were noted: Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Item 6f does not appear to be the first box that applies if the petitioner is the decedent's sister. Item 8 of the petition is missing the relationships to the decedent of the persons listed (but deceased persons have addresses?). The date of death of any deceased heir must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] There are disclaimers attached to the petition. Who are the heirs of the disclaimants? If the disclaiming parties have issue, they would take in the place of the disclaiming heir. [Prob. C. Sec. 282.] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/29/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
